actions.title: Email admissions office with questions
actions.templateContent:
Subject: Prospective Student Inquiry - MBA/MS Program

Dear Cornell Tech Admissions Team,

I hope this email finds you well. My name is Ian Madrid, and I am very interested in applying to the Cornell Tech MBA/MS program for Fall 2027.

I have been researching the program extensively and am particularly drawn to the unique combination of business education with hands-on technology focus at the Roosevelt Island campus.

I would appreciate some clarification on the following:

1. What is the typical GMAT/GRE score range for admitted students in recent cohorts?
2. Are there any upcoming information sessions or virtual events where I can learn more about the program culture?
3. For the MS specialization, how flexible is the course selection across different tech focus areas?
4. What percentage of students typically receive merit-based scholarships?

I understand you receive many inquiries, and I truly appreciate your time and guidance. Please let me know if there are any additional resources you'd recommend for prospective students.

Thank you for your assistance.

Best regards,
Ian Madrid
[Your Phone Number]
[Your LinkedIn Profile (optional)]

actions.title: Request recommendation letter from Prof. Smith
actions.templateContent:
Subject: Request for Letter of Recommendation - Cornell Tech MBA/MS

Dear Professor Smith,

I hope you're doing well! I'm reaching out because I'm applying to the Cornell Tech MBA/MS program, and I would be honored if you could write a letter of recommendation on my behalf.

I thoroughly enjoyed your [Course Name] class in [Semester/Year], where I [mention specific project/achievement - e.g., "led the team project on X and earned an A"]. Your class significantly influenced my decision to pursue further education at the intersection of business and technology.

The Cornell Tech program requires recommendations that speak to:
- Analytical and quantitative abilities
- Leadership potential
- Ability to work in team environments
- Why I'm a good fit for their program

The deadline for submission is October 1st, and the recommendation will be submitted online through their application portal.

I completely understand if you're too busy or don't feel you can write a strong recommendation. If you're willing, I'd be happy to:
- Meet during office hours to discuss my goals
- Provide my resume and statement of purpose draft
- Share more details about the program

Thank you so much for considering this request. Your mentorship has meant a lot to me, and I'd be grateful for your support.

Best regards,
Ian Madrid
[Your Student ID (if recent grad)]
[Your Phone Number]
[Your Email]

actions.title: Request recommendation letter from current employer
actions.templateContent:
Subject: Request for Letter of Recommendation - MBA Program Application

Dear [Manager's Name],

I hope you're having a great week! I'm writing to share some exciting news and ask for your support.

I've decided to apply to the Cornell Tech MBA/MS program, a dual degree that combines business education with technology focus. This aligns perfectly with my career goals of [mention your goal - e.g., "leading tech-driven transformations in the financial services industry"], and I believe it will significantly enhance my ability to contribute to our team and the broader organization.

As part of the application, I need to submit 2-3 professional recommendations. Given our work together on [specific project/initiative], I can't think of anyone better positioned to speak to my:
- Leadership and problem-solving abilities
- Impact on [specific project/team outcome]
- Potential for growth in business and technology leadership

The recommendation is submitted online and should take about 15-20 minutes to complete. The deadline is October 1st.

I completely understand if you're swamped or if this isn't the right time. If you're willing, I'd love to:
- Chat briefly about my goals and why this program
- Share my resume and a summary of my key contributions here
- Provide any other information that would be helpful

Thank you so much for considering this. Your support would mean a lot to me, and I'm committed to ensuring this doesn't add significant burden to your schedule.

Best regards,
Ian
[Your Title]
[Your Phone Number]

actions.title: Draft statement of purpose
actions.templateContent:
STATEMENT OF PURPOSE - OUTLINE
================================

CORNELL TECH MBA/MS PROGRAM

---

PARAGRAPH 1: THE HOOK (Why This Moment?)
- Start with a specific moment that defined your interest in tech + business
- Example: "When I scaled our workforce from 4 agents to 3,000+, I realized technology wasn't just a tool—it was the multiplier."
- Connect to why NOW is the right time for this degree

PARAGRAPH 2: YOUR BACKGROUND (What You've Built)
- Brief overview of your journey
- Key achievements: CEO of SP Madrid (3,000+ person workforce), Founder of OkPo & Convinced AI, President of Texxen
- Highlight the intersection of business leadership + technology adoption
- Mention your TikTok presence (65K followers) - shows you understand modern tech platforms

PARAGRAPH 3: THE GAP (What You're Missing)
- Be honest about what you don't know
- Example: "I've built businesses, but I want to build the NEXT generation of businesses—ones powered by AI and built for global scale."
- Connect to specific skills: [technical depth, product development, scaling tech companies]

PARAGRAPH 4: WHY CORNELL TECH (Why This Program?)
- SPECIFIC to Cornell Tech (not generic MBA reasons)
- Mention: Roosevelt Island campus, industry partnerships, Studio work
- Specific professors or courses you're excited about
- The dual degree structure (MBA + MS) - why both matter to you
- NYC location and tech ecosystem

PARAGRAPH 5: YOUR CONTRIBUTUTION (What You Bring)
- You're not just a student—you're a practitioner
- Real-world experience scaling companies
- Global perspective (Philippines, Singapore, Dubai operations)
- Understanding of emerging markets and tech adoption
- Willingness to share lessons with classmates

PARAGRAPH 6: POST-MBA GOALS (What's Next?)
- Short-term: [Specific role/industry/company type]
- Long-term: [Your 10-year vision]
- How Cornell Tech specifically enables this
- Why an MBA/MS is essential (not just nice to have)

PARAGRAPH 7: THE CLOSE (Why You?)
- Reiterate fit with Cornell Tech's mission
- End with confidence but not arrogance
- Example: "I'm ready to contribute, learn, and build the future alongside the Cornell Tech community."

---

WORD COUNT TARGET: 750-1000 words
TONE: Authentic, specific, forward-looking
AVOID: Generic MBA essays, clichés, vague statements

KEY DIFFERENTIATORS FOR YOU:
✓ Built from 4 people to 3,000+ (real scaling experience)
✓ Multiple companies across different sectors
✓ Global operations (PH, SG, Dubai)
✓ Content creator (65K TikTok) - understand modern tech platforms
✓ Builder mentality, not just manager

---

NEXT STEPS:
1. Write rough draft following this outline (don't edit, just write)
2. Let it sit for 2-3 days
3. Come back and revise for clarity and impact
4. Get feedback from 2-3 trusted people
5. Final polish for grammar and flow

actions.title: Research housing options near Roosevelt Island

costs.note: Can waive if you have qualifying insurance
Fee waivers available for qualified programs (Forte, McNair, etc.)

costs.description: Brief stay in Ithaca for orientation/immersion

scholarships.name: Cornell Tech Merit Scholarship
scholarships.requirements: Automatic consideration with application - holistic review of credentials
scholarships.note: Apply by priority deadline for best chance. No separate application needed.

scholarships.name: Forté Fellowship
scholarships.requirements: Women demonstrating leadership potential in business
scholarships.note: Must apply by priority deadline. Listed in fee waiver programs.

scholarships.name: NSF Graduate Research Fellowship (GRFP)
scholarships.note: Highly competitive - requires research proposal and 3 references

scholarships.name: Application Fee Waiver
scholarships.requirements: Must be participant in listed programs (Forte, McNair, GEM, SACNAS, etc.)
scholarships.note: Email admissions@tech.cornell.edu 3 days before deadline to request

scholarships.name: Federal Student Loans
scholarships.note: Need-based aid not available from Cornell Tech, but federal loans available

scholarships.name: External Scholarships
scholarships.note: Examples: GEM Fellowship, NSBE, SHPE, SWE - target $10-20K combined

research.title: Program Structure
research.content: Johnson Cornell Tech MBA is a 1-year intensive program (NOT 2 years). Located at Cornell Tech campus on Roosevelt Island, NYC. Combines core MBA curriculum with tech-focused studios and projects. Includes brief stay in Ithaca for immersion.

research.title: Tuition & Fees (Official 2025-2026)
research.content: Tuition: $135,374 | New Student Fee: $1,500 (one-time) | Health Insurance: ~$4,000 (can waive) | NYC Health Fee: $340 | Student Activity Fee: $110 | Living Expenses: $38,027/year | TOTAL: ~$179,351

research.title: Application Deadlines (Official)
research.content: Priority Deadline: Oct 8, 2025 (11:59 AM EST) - Best for scholarships | Round 2: Jan 8, 2026 (11:59 AM EST) - Main round | Rolling/ Round 3: Mar 5, 2026 (11:59 AM EST) - Final deadline, limited aid

research.title: Scholarships
research.content: Cornell Tech offers merit-based scholarships (no separate application). Determined by holistic review at admission. Apply by priority deadline (Oct 8) for best chance. Scholarships cover tuition only, not housing. Full-tuition funding NOT available, but significant portions possible. Fee waivers available for Forte, McNair, GEM, SACNAS, NSBE, SHPE, SWE participants.

research.title: Housing
research.content: The House at Cornell Tech - steps from campus on Roosevelt Island. Furnished apartments, modern amenities. Also need brief housing in Ithaca for orientation/immersion (~1 week).

research.title: Health Insurance
research.content: All students required to have insurance meeting Cornell's minimum. Automatically enrolled in Cornell SHP (~$4,000/year). International students MUST use Cornell SHP. US citizens/permanent residents can waive if they have qualifying insurance.
